title: phanera bidentata var. gracilipes
intro: Phanera bidentata var. gracilipes, also known as the climbing rattan or climbing palm, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Phanera bidentata var. gracilipes include its potential to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues, as well as its use as a treatment for snake bites and other venomous injuries. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's stem and roots are typically used to make infusions or decoctions, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically for wound healing.
